topic: Feasibility study vs business plan — what Development Bank Oman actually wants from you
format: Open with a question about a business idea the reader has been sitting on, then show the exact first step to fund it
post:
You have a business idea. Maybe it's been living in your notes app for six months, waiting.

The thing blocking you isn't the idea. It's not knowing what the bank actually needs from you.

Here's where most first-time applicants get tripped up: Development Bank Oman doesn't ask for a business plan. They ask for a feasibility study. These are not the same document.

A business plan is your vision. A feasibility study is evidence — market size, projected cash flow, cost structure, break-even point. It shows the bank whether your idea can survive contact with reality.

Most applicants arrive with a Word document that mixes both and satisfies neither.

The Sanad office staff will tell you this themselves: incomplete or misformatted feasibility studies are the number one reason applications stall before anyone even reads them.

You don't need a consultant charging OMR 300 to sort this out. You need the right structure from day one.

DBO runs 10 funding programs. Each has different eligibility criteria. Knowing which one actually fits your business before you submit — that changes your odds significantly.

What's the business you've been sitting on?

Build a bank-ready feasibility study free in ~10 minutes — covers all 10 Development Bank Oman / Riyada programs: https://www.thesmartpro.io/feasibility-studio?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=feasibility
